rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port assumptions/guarantees, and implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE content, with type scopes for constants and equations, implementation scopes for assignments.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE expressions use only visible identifiers and preserve type consistency.
Use valid AGREE syntax: assume, guarantee, eq, const, assign with proper types.
Avoid referencing implementation-only subcomponents in component type contracts.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks.
Use eq for helper declarations and const for named constants with explicit types.
Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence patterns.
Branching logic should use nested if-then-else expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not reference AADL properties as AGREE variables without explicit model exposure.
Do not invent new identifiers; only use visible features from the current scope.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing parameters
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair actions
Model unit and package qualification in AGREE expressions
